caption: A technical presentation slide titled "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" and "Tutorial – 4" is shown, focusing on the calculation of "Liquid yield." The slide features a mathematical formula for liquid yield, $y$, and a data table with parameters for states "1", "2", "f", and "g," including pressure $p$ in bar, temperature $T$ in Kelvin, enthalpy $h$ in j/g, and entropy $s$ in j/gK. For example, the table lists $T$ as 300 K for states 1 and 2, and 77 K for states f and g. Below the table, a numerical substitution into the formula is displayed as $(462 - 430) - (1 - 0.90)(462 - 230)$ divided by $(462 - 29) - (1 - 0.90)(462 - 230)$. Throughout the sequence, a white cursor moves across the screen. In the final frames, the cursor highlights the result "= 0.021". The footer attributes the content to "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" and includes an "NPTEL" logo.
